sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: First Community Bankshares completes acquisition of Hometown Bancshares By Investing.com
feed.summary: First Community Bankshares, Inc. has completed its acquisition of Hometown Bancshares, Inc. and its subsidiary, Union Bank, Inc., adding eight West Virginia branch locations. The transaction, valued at $624.54 million in market capitalization, brings approximately $415 million in assets, $172 million in loans, and $376 million in deposits to First Community. This move aligns with First Community's strategy to grow low-cost core deposits and expand its presence in the Parkersburg-Marietta-Vienna metropolitan area.

feed.title: Brown & Brown Inc. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: Brown & Brown Inc. (BRO) stock declined by 6.91% on Tuesday, closing at $74.12. This underperformance occurred on a mixed day for the broader market, with the S&P 500 Index rising and the Dow Jones Industrial Average falling. The decline ended a three-day winning streak for Brown & Brown Inc.

feed.title: Corning: Meta Deal Greatly Expands Our Fiber Growth Expectations
feed.summary: Corning and Meta Platforms have signed a long-term supply agreement for optical fiber cable, projected to reach up to $6 billion in sales by 2030, leading Morningstar to significantly raise its fair value estimate for Corning stock. This deal is expected to materially increase Corning's fiber growth expectations, with a new forecast of $10 billion in new annual revenue by 2030, compared to 2025. Despite the positive outlook, Morningstar has increased Corning's Uncertainty Rating to High due to AI spending risks, noting that the stock appears modestly overvalued given its recent performance.

feed.title: Jack Henry Announces Fiscal 2026 Second Quarter Deconversion Revenue Results
feed.summary: Jack Henry & Associates, Inc. announced that its deconversion revenue for the fiscal second quarter, ended December 31, 2025, reached $6.2 million. This led to an increase in deconversion revenue estimates to $28 million for the full fiscal year 2026. The company clarifies that deconversion revenue, largely from clients being acquired, is excluded from non-GAAP revenue as it does not reflect the company's core operations.

feed.title: Apple supplier Qorvo’s stock slumps after hours on soft guidance
feed.summary: Qorvo Inc.'s stock declined in extended trading after the Apple supplier issued weaker-than-expected guidance for the current quarter, citing a seasonal decline from its largest customer, Apple. Despite beating analyst estimates for fiscal Q3 2026 EPS and revenue, the radio-frequency chipmaker's Q4 outlook fell short, leading to a 7.9% drop in share price. Qorvo is in the process of being acquired by Skyworks Solutions Inc.

feed.title: Prediction: Super Micro Could Join the $1 Trillion Club by 2035
feed.summary: This article suggests that despite current slowing growth, Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ: SMCI) has the potential to reach a $1 trillion valuation by 2035 due to its expanding role in AI infrastructure and edge computing, new partnerships, and favorable analyst targets. The Motley Fool's Stock Advisor team, however, did not include SMCI in their current top 10 stock recommendations.

feed.title: Con Edison Declares Quarterly Dividend Increase
feed.summary: Con Edison has announced a quarterly dividend increase to 88.75 cents per share, marking its 52nd consecutive annual dividend raise, the longest streak among S&P 500 utilities. The company aims for a dividend payout ratio of 55% to 65% of adjusted earnings. Wall Street analysts currently forecast a slight fall in ED's stock price over the next 12 months, with an average target of $101.18.

feed.title: Consolidated Edison Raises Quarterly Dividend to $0.8875 a Share, Payable March 16 to Holders of Record Feb. 18
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison (ED) has announced an increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.8875 per share. The dividend is payable on March 16 to shareholders of record as of February 18. The article also provides recent analyst ratings and price targets for the utility company.

feed.title: CF Industries Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.50 a Share, Payable Feb. 27 to Shareholders of Record Feb. 13
feed.summary: CF Industries (CF) announced it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.50 per share. The dividend is scheduled to be paid on February 27 to shareholders of record as of February 13. This information was released on January 27, 2026.

feed.title: F5 (NASDAQ:FFIV) Releases Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $0.82 EPS
feed.summary: F5 (NASDAQ:FFIV) announced strong earnings results, reporting $4.45 EPS, significantly beating the consensus estimate of $3.63 by $0.82. The company also surpassed revenue estimates with $822.47 million against a $755.97 million consensus, demonstrating robust profitability with a 21.76% ROE and a 22.42% net margin. Following the announcement, F5's stock traded higher, rising $2.98 to $270.43, and analysts maintain an average "Hold" rating with a price target of $299.78.

feed.title: Eversource Energy approves quarterly dividend of $0.7875 per share
feed.summary: Eversource Energy announced a quarterly dividend of $0.7875 per share, payable on March 31, 2026, marking its 27th consecutive year of dividend payments. This news comes amidst financial challenges for the utility company, including the rejection of a $1.6 billion sale and subsequent downgrades by analysts, yet Eversource maintains its financial outlook and commitment to energy efficiency.

feed.title: Chevron appoints former American Airlines chairman to board
feed.summary: Chevron Corporation has appointed Thomas W. Horton, former chairman and CEO of American Airlines, to its Board of Directors as an Independent Director, where he will serve on the Board Audit Committee. Horton brings extensive board experience from his roles at Walmart, General Electric, Qualcomm, and Enlink Midstream. This appointment comes as Chevron continues to expand its global energy operations and initiatives.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Guides Above Q1 Estimates After Roughly In-Line Q4
feed.summary: Chipmaker Texas Instruments (TXN) slightly missed Wall Street's fourth-quarter earnings and sales targets but provided first-quarter guidance that exceeded expectations. The company reported earnings of $1.27 per share on sales of $4.42 billion for the December quarter, compared to analyst expectations of $1.29 per share and a similar sales figure. TXN stock saw a rise in extended trading following the announcement.

feed.title: WesBanco earnings missed by $0.01, revenue topped estimates
feed.summary: WesBanco (NASDAQ: WSBC) reported fourth-quarter EPS of $0.84, missing analyst estimates by $0.01. However, the company's revenue of $265.57 million slightly topped the consensus estimate of $265.53 million. Despite the EPS miss, WesBanco's stock has seen positive performance over the last three months and has a "fair performance" financial health score according to InvestingPro.

feed.title: Copart Inc Is Quietly Printing Money – Here’s Why Everyone’s Watching CPRT Right Now
feed.summary: Copart Inc. (CPRT) is highlighted as a robust, albeit low-profile, player in the salvage-car auction market. The article emphasizes its resilient business model, operating as a digital-first auction platform with extensive physical yards for damaged vehicles. It attracts a niche but strong following among car-flippers and rebuilders, positioning itself as an essential infrastructure component in the car-resale ecosystem.

feed.title: The Truth About Hewlett Packard Enterprise: Is This ‘Boring’ Stock About To Go Viral?
feed.summary: This article discusses Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) as a potentially undervalued tech stock, focusing on its role in AI infrastructure, hybrid cloud, and recurring revenue models. It highlights HPE's steady, low-drama profile compared to more volatile tech stocks and examines its competitive landscape against Dell, cloud giants, and Cisco. The piece concludes that HPE is a "cop" for long-term, infrastructure-focused investors seeking stability rather than rapid, hype-driven gains.

feed.title: F5 Networks surges 13% on beat and raise
feed.summary: F5 Networks (NASDAQ:FFIV) shares jumped 13% in extended trading after reporting a strong first-quarter earnings and revenue beat. The company also raised its full-year outlook, citing robust product demand and higher margins. Non-GAAP earnings and revenue significantly surpassed analyst estimates, driven by demand for hybrid multicloud deployments and AI adoption.

feed.title: CON EDISON DECLARES COMMON STOCK DIVIDEND
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison, Inc. (Con Edison) has declared a quarterly dividend of 88.75 cents per share on its common stock, payable March 16, 2026, marking its 52nd consecutive annual dividend increase. This increase reflects the company's commitment to investor returns and providing reliable service during the clean energy transition, targeting a dividend payout ratio between 55% and 65% of its adjusted earnings. Con Edison is a holding company with subsidiaries providing electric, gas, and steam services in New York and New Jersey, and investing in electric transmission projects.

feed.title: Saratoga Investment Signs Multiple Material Agreements
feed.summary: Saratoga Investment Corp. (SAR) has signed two material agreements concerning its new 7.25% Senior Unsecured Notes due 2030. The company completed a $50 million private placement and entered a Registration Rights Agreement to facilitate an exchange offer for registered notes within 365 days. The net proceeds of $48.5 million will be used to redeem its 4.375% Notes due 2026, improving liquidity and extending maturity dates.

feed.title: Raymond James launches AI operations agent to enhance service
feed.summary: Raymond James Financial has launched Rai, a proprietary AI operations agent that uses natural language processing and generative AI to provide immediate answers to operational questions. The tool, which adapts to user activities and leverages firm knowledge bases, will be rolled out firm-wide after a successful pilot. This initiative is part of Raymond James's broader investment in technology and AI, demonstrating its commitment to enhancing service models and empowering financial advisors.

feed.title: Southwest Airlines Reinvents Seating Model And Revenue Story For Investors
feed.summary: Southwest Airlines is implementing a major shift from its long-standing open seating model to assigned seating, introducing premium options and a segmented pricing structure. This strategic overhaul, which aims to diversify revenue streams through higher-priced seats and new fare tiers, will redefine the customer experience and reposition the airline within the industry. Investors will be closely watching customer reception, premium fare adoption, and the impact on unit revenue, while also monitoring execution risks related to technology and operations.

feed.title: HPE Taps 2degrees For Private AI Push And New Zealand Data Sovereignty
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) and New Zealand telco 2degrees have partnered to build a private AI platform using HPE Private Cloud AI, focusing on AI-driven analytics, secure on-premises cloud infrastructure, and data sovereignty. This collaboration aims to enhance 2degrees' network reliability and data security for local customers, providing HPE with a crucial reference case in a regulated sector. The initiative highlights HPE's strategy to align its cloud offerings with regional digital sovereignty priorities and its role as a key AI infrastructure partner.

feed.title: Alliance Resource Partners, L.P. Declares Quarterly Distribution of $0.60 Per Unit
feed.summary: Alliance Resource Partners, L.P. (ARLP) announced a quarterly cash distribution of $0.60 per unit for the quarter ended December 31, 2025, payable on February 13, 2026, to unitholders of record as of February 6, 2026. The company will report its financial results for the 2025 quarter on February 2, 2026, and will hold a conference call to discuss these results. ARLP also provided guidance to brokers regarding federal income tax withholding for non-U.S. investors on these distributions.

feed.title: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. Declares Quarterly Dividend and Confirms Dates for Fourth Quarter 2025 Results and Conference Call
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: CF) has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share, payable on February 27, 2026, to stockholders of record as of February 13, 2026. The company also announced it would release its fourth quarter and full-year 2025 results after market close on February 18, 2026, followed by a conference call on February 19, 2026, at 11:00 a.m. ET to discuss these results.

feed.title: Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. Appoints Dr. Homaira Akbari (Ph.D.) to Board of Directors
feed.summary: Babcock & Wilcox Enterprises, Inc. (B&W) has announced the appointment of Dr. Homaira Akbari (Ph.D.) to its Board of Directors, increasing the board to seven members. Dr. Akbari brings extensive international leadership experience from various technology, energy, and finance sectors, having previously served on over 25 public and private company boards and as a member of B&W's Advisory Board. Her expertise in AI factories, data centers, and power generation systems is expected to significantly contribute to B&W's strategic initiatives in power solutions, carbon capture, and environmental technologies.

feed.title: PPG Industries falls as Q4 earnings miss overshadows revenue beat
feed.summary: PPG Industries (NYSE:PPG) shares dropped over 2% after the paint and coatings manufacturer reported fourth-quarter adjusted earnings per share of $1.51, missing analyst estimates of $1.58. Despite the earnings miss, the company's revenue of $3.91 billion exceeded expectations, growing 5% year-over-year, driven by strong organic sales growth across all regions. PPG anticipates adjusted EPS for full-year 2026 to be between $7.70 and $8.10, with organic sales growth expected to be flat to low single-digit percentages.

feed.title: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. Announces Participation in Upcoming Investor Conferences
feed.summary: J.B. Hunt Transport Services, Inc. announced that its executives will participate in three upcoming investor conferences in February and March 2026. Brad Delco will host meetings at the Evercore ISI Travel & Transport Conference, Brad Hicks and Brad Delco will address the Barclays 43rd Annual Industrial Select Conference, and Shelley Simpson and Nick Hobbs will speak at the Raymond James 47th Annual Institutional Investors Conference. Investors can access live presentations and replays via the company's Investor Relations website.

feed.title: Invesco Ltd (IVZ) Q4 2025 Earnings Call Highlights: Record AUM and Strategic Partnerships Drive Growth
feed.summary: Invesco Ltd (IVZ) reported a strong Q4 2025 with $2.2 trillion in Assets Under Management (AUM), an 18% increase year-over-year, and significant net long-term inflows of $19 billion. The company achieved a 6% growth in net revenues for 2025, driven by record ETF and index investment revenues, and successfully recapitalized its balance sheet through a $1.5 billion preferred stock repurchase. Despite challenges like outflows in fundamental equities and anticipated higher costs for its hybrid investment platform, Invesco plans to continue share buybacks and leverage strategic partnerships for future growth.

feed.title: Informa TechTarget to Announce Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Financial Results on March 11, 2026
feed.summary: Informa TechTarget (Nasdaq: TTGT) announced it will release its financial results for Q4 and full-year 2025 after market close on Wednesday, March 11, 2026. CEO Gary Nugent and CFO Dan Noreck will host a conference call and webcast at 5:00 p.m. ET on the same day to discuss the results and outlook. Financial results will be posted on the company's investor relations website prior to the call.

feed.title: Radware acquires Israeli API security startup Pynt to bolster cloud protection
feed.summary: Radware has acquired Pynt, an Israeli API security startup, to enhance its cloud protection offerings. This deal integrates pre-production API security testing into Radware's existing platform, aiming to identify and fix security risks earlier in the development lifecycle. Key Pynt employees, including its founders, have joined Radware, and Pynt's technology will remain available as both a standalone solution and as part of Radware's integrated security portfolio.

feed.title: SKYX Platforms Corp. (SKYX) Is Up 16.21% in One Week: What You Should Know
feed.summary: SKYX Platforms Corp. (SKYX) is highlighted as a strong momentum pick, with its stock up 16.21% in the past week and 22.87% monthly, significantly outperforming the Zacks Technology Services industry. The company currently holds a Zacks Rank of #2 (Buy) and a Momentum Style Score of A, indicating potential for continued market outperformance. Positive earnings estimate revisions further support its bullish outlook.

feed.title: Chipotle plans to do a lot more with its menu this year
feed.summary: Chipotle is dramatically increasing its menu innovation this year after a challenging 2025. The fast-casual chain will bring back popular limited-time offers like Chicken al Pastor on February 10th, and plans several new proteins, sides, and dips. This strategy aims to boost sales by introducing a mix of new and proven successful items, which has shown to increase customer visits and spending.

feed.title: Texas Instruments (NASDAQ:TXN) Releases Quarterly Earnings Results, Misses Estimates By $0.01 EPS
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (NASDAQ:TXN) reported Q earnings of $1.27 EPS, falling short of analysts' consensus estimates of $1.28 by $0.01, with revenue of $4.42 billion against an expected $4.44 billion. Despite missing estimates, the company maintained strong profitability with a net margin of 29.21% and ROE of 30.41%. TXN declared a quarterly dividend of $1.42, resulting in a 2.9% yield, but its high payout ratio of 103.84% indicates payouts exceed current earnings.

feed.title: One Of Chipotle's Most Requested Menu Items Returning: When You Can Get It
feed.summary: Chipotle's popular Chicken al Pastor is returning to its menu in February 2026 for a limited time across its global locations. This fan-favorite protein, which first debuted in March 2023, will be available starting February 10, with early access for Rewards members on February 9. The company also plans to introduce new protein-focused menu items throughout 2026, building on the success of limited-time offerings.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Sales Rise, But Profit Slips
feed.summary: Texas Instruments reported a rise in revenue but a slight dip in profit for the fiscal fourth quarter. The semiconductor company posted $1.16 billion in profit, or $1.27 per share, on revenue of $4.42 billion, which was a 10% increase year-over-year. The company provided first-quarter revenue guidance between $4.32 billion and $4.68 billion, with earnings per share projected from $1.22 to $1.48.

feed.title: Seagate Q2 Beats Earnings and Revenue Estimates, Strong Guidance
feed.summary: Seagate Technology Holdings (NASDAQ: STX) exceeded its Q2 earnings and revenue estimates, reporting strong financial results. The company posted quarterly earnings of $3.11 per share and revenue of $2.83 billion, surpassing analyst expectations. Seagate also provided robust guidance for Q3, projecting adjusted EPS of $3.20 to $3.60 and revenue between $2.8 billion and $3 billion.

feed.title: F5 surges after Q1 results topple estimates on 11% product revenue growth
feed.summary: F5 (FFIV) shares surged 12% in post-market trading after reporting strong Q1 fiscal 2026 financial results. The company exceeded earnings per share estimates, driven by an 11% growth in product revenue. This positive performance has led to a significant increase in its stock price.

feed.title: McDonald's brings back an iconic item from the 1980s
feed.summary: McDonald's is reintroducing its beloved "Changeables" Happy Meal toys from the 1980s and 90s, with 16 updated robot and dinosaur designs. This move is part of the fast-food giant's broader strategy to leverage nostalgia, technology, and loyalty initiatives to attract customers amid economic pressures and increased competition, following similar value-focused offerings like the Extra Value Meals.

feed.title: SK Telecom Co., Ltd.'s (KRX:017670) P/E Is Still On The Mark Following 31% Share Price Bounce
feed.summary: SK Telecom Co., Ltd.'s stock has seen a significant 31% surge in the last month, yet its price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio of 26.1x remains elevated compared to the broader Korean market. Despite a recent decline in earnings (down 49% in the last year and 34% over three years), analysts forecast a robust 32% annual earnings growth for the next three years, outperforming the market's 21% prediction. This optimistic outlook on future earnings growth is cited as the primary reason for the company's high P/E ratio, suggesting investor confidence despite past performance.

feed.title: Ellington Financial launches 8.7 million share offering to redeem preferred stock
feed.summary: Ellington Financial Inc. announced a public offering of 8.775 million shares of common stock to redeem its Series A Preferred Stock. The company, currently trading near its 52-week high, plans to use remaining proceeds for general corporate purposes and potential acquisitions, highlighting its consistent dividend payments and recent financial performance. Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs are joint book-running managers for the offering.

feed.title: Boeing Earnings Trounce Expectations. The Stock Is All Over the Place.
feed.summary: Boeing (BA) reported earnings that significantly exceeded expectations, causing volatility in its stock price. Despite the strong earnings, the stock was up 16% year-to-date, outperforming the S&P 500 by a considerable margin. The article details the market's reaction to Boeing's financial performance.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (WSBC) WesBanco, Inc. Reports Q4 Revenue $262.5M, vs. FactSet Est of $265.5M
feed.summary: WesBanco, Inc. (WSBC) reported its Q4 revenue at $262.5 million, falling short of FactSet's estimate of $265.5 million. The report provides a quick financial update for the company, highlighting its revenue performance against analyst expectations. Further details on earnings and other financial metrics may be available in more comprehensive reports.

feed.title: Assessing Ventas (VTR) Valuation As Earnings Growth And Cash Flow Support An Upward Share Price Trend
feed.summary: Ventas (VTR) is gaining investor interest due to strong earnings growth, consistent revenue, and expanded free cash flow, which align with an upward share price trend. The article indicates a fair value of $85.65, suggesting Ventas is currently undervalued at its trading price of $76.28, despite its P/S ratio being higher than the industry average. However, potential risks like reliance on acquisitions and labor costs could challenge this optimistic outlook.

feed.title: Why Vertex (VRTX) Is Up 8.0% After Analyst Upgrades Spotlight Its CF Strength And Kidney Pipeline
feed.summary: Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) recently saw its stock rise by 8.0% following a series of analyst upgrades. These upgrades highlighted the continued strength of its cystic fibrosis portfolio and the promising potential of its kidney disease pipeline, particularly povetacicept's FDA orphan drug status. While the core investment narrative remains its high-margin CF franchise funding rare-disease and gene-editing platforms, the new enthusiasm around povetacicept adds a significant kidney-disease pillar to its growth story, with upcoming Q4 2025 results on February 12th serving as a near-term catalyst.

feed.title: Texas Instruments Misses Q4 Estimates, Shares Surge As Q1 Guidance Shines
feed.summary: Texas Instruments reported its fourth-quarter financial results, missing revenue and earnings estimates. Despite the Q4 miss, shares surged in after-hours trading due to strong first-quarter guidance that exceeded analyst expectations. The company provided an optimistic outlook for Q1 revenue and earnings per share, driving investor confidence.

feed.title: Texas Instruments perks up amid Q4 results, guidance (TXN:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Texas Instruments (TXN) shares rose 5.5% in extended trading after reporting fiscal fourth-quarter results and guidance that largely met Wall Street's expectations. The analog semiconductor giant announced earnings of $1.27 per share for the period ending December 31.

feed.title: HP Inc. Declares Dividend
feed.summary: HP Inc. (NYSE: HPQ) has declared a cash dividend of $0.3000 per share on its common stock, marking the second dividend of fiscal year 2026. This dividend is payable on April 1, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 11, 2026. HP Inc. is a global technology leader focusing on AI-powered devices, software, services, and subscriptions, with approximately 0.9 billion shares outstanding.

feed.title: Citi’s HR department ‘spearheaded’ a harassment campaign, former managing director alleges
feed.summary: A former managing director at Citigroup has filed a lawsuit alleging that the company's HR department spearheaded a "harassment campaign" against her, including "baseless" investigations that ruined her reputation and led to her constructive discharge. The lawsuit claims a "deeply misogynistic" culture at the firm and on Wall Street, enabled by forced arbitration until the 2022 Ending Forced Arbitration of Sexual Assault and Sexual Harassment Act. Citi has denied the allegations, stating they have "absolutely no merit."

feed.title: HP Inc. (HPQ) Declares $0.3000 Quarterly Dividend; 6.2% Yield
feed.summary: HP Inc. (HPQ) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.3000 per share, leading to an annualized dividend of $1.2 and an annual yield of 6.2 percent. The dividend is payable on April 1, 2026, to stockholders of record by March 11, 2026, with the ex-dividend date being March 10, 2026. This announcement provides shareholders with details on the company's dividend payout.

feed.title: SAFX XCF Global up 110% to $0.29 on NASDAQ after hours 27 Jan 2026: heavy volume
feed.summary: SAFX XCF Global stock surged 110.42% to $0.29 on NASDAQ after-hours on January 27, 2026, driven by high volume and reports of production updates from its New Rise Reno SAF facility. Despite the significant price jump and oversold technical indicators, Meyka AI issues a cautious "HOLD" rating with a projected one-month downside of 34.25% to $0.19, citing mixed fundamentals including low liquidity and high debt, alongside the speculative nature of the trading. Active traders are advised to use strict risk controls due to high volatility, while investors should monitor production milestones and cash flow before adjusting exposure.
